title: Podcast EP354: How Siemens EDA is Conquering New Lithography Challenges with Sagar Saxena - Semiwiki
gist: A SemiWiki podcast summary describes Siemens EDA's move from orthogonal (Manhattan-style) mask layouts to curvilinear mask formats, which are more compatible with high-density manufacturing but introduce significant OPC/geometry-processing challenges around run time, data size, and complexity.

insights.claim: Mask fabrication is shifting from orthogonal (Manhattan-style) layout to a curvilinear format, which is more compatible with current high-density semiconductor manufacturing.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Signals a structural shift in lithography/mask technology across advanced nodes, relevant for EDA vendors and mask/OPC suppliers competing for share as curvilinear becomes standard.
insights.quote: Sagar describes the changes underway to move from orthogonal (Manhattan-style) mask layout to a curvilinear format.
insights.locator: Dan explores the changing world of mask fabrication

insights.claim: Curvilinear mask geometries introduce substantial challenges for geometry processing and optical proximity correction (OPC), including run time, data size, and overall complexity.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Highlights a technical bottleneck (compute cost, data volume) that creates demand for specialized EDA/computational-lithography tooling — a competitive opportunity for vendors who solve it first.
insights.quote: Sagar explains that curvilinear shapes are more compatible with current high-density manufacturing, but these types of geometries introduce substantial challenges for geometry processing and OPC. These challenges include run time, data size and overall complexity.
insights.locator: Dan explores the changing world of mask fabrication

insights.claim: Siemens EDA has implemented a distinct approach to address curvilinear OPC challenges that opens the way to better yield and performance, with actual customer usage already described.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Indicates Siemens EDA already has customer traction on curvilinear OPC, positioning it competitively against Synopsys and Cadence in the computational lithography segment of the EDA market.
insights.quote: Sagar describes a unique approach that Siemens EDA has implemented to address these challenges, opening the way to better yield and performance. He describes the approach, along with descriptions of actual customer usage.
